nodes.title: Validate the prototype before creating anything

anchor.snippet: const validProto = proto === null || typeof proto === 'object' || typeof proto === 'function';

nodes.why: Only objects, functions, or `null` make sense as a prototype target.
nodes.alternative: Let `Object.create` throw on bad input by itself.
nodes.tradeoff: Clearer error intent, with one explicit validation step.

nodes.title: Use `Object.create` for prototype wiring
nodes.why: This is the built-in way to create an object with a specific prototype.
nodes.alternative: Use a temporary constructor function pattern.
nodes.tradeoff: Short and correct, but depends on the native helper being available.

nodes.title: Copy optional own properties after creation
nodes.why: Prototype wiring and own-property assignment are separate concerns, so this keeps them cleanly split.
nodes.alternative: Ignore `props` completely or define descriptors instead.
nodes.tradeoff: Simple shallow property copy, but not a descriptor-level clone.
